inputs.userGoal: Launch a data ingestion pipeline using Azure Data Factory for importing sales data daily, transform using Azure Databricks, and store results in Azure Synapse Analytics

reasoning.whyChosen: Data Lake Storage Gen2 provides hierarchical namespace for efficient big data analytics, integrates seamlessly with Data Factory and Synapse Analytics. Hot tier optimized for frequent access of daily sales data.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Slightly higher cost than cool tier but required for frequent daily processing

references.title: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 overview

resources.name: adf-sales-etl-prod-eastus2-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure Data Factory provides managed data integration service for orchestrating daily sales data ingestion with built-in connectors, scheduling capabilities, and monitoring.
reasoning.tradeoffs: More complex than Logic Apps but provides better data transformation capabilities, monitoring, and enterprise-grade connectors

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires storage account for staging data during pipeline execution and as a data source/sink

resources.name: dbw-sales-analytics-prod-eastus2-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure Databricks provides unified analytics platform with Apache Spark for complex data transformations. Excellent integration with Data Factory for automated pipeline execution and advanced analytics capabilities.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Higher cost than Synapse Spark but provides better collaborative environment, notebook experience, and optimized Spark runtime

resources.dependencyReasoning: Reads raw sales data from storage account and writes transformed data back to storage

resources.name: synw-sales-analytics-prod-eastus2-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure Synapse Analytics provides integrated analytics service combining data warehousing, big data analytics, and data integration. Optimized for analytical workloads on large datasets.
reasoning.tradeoffs: More complex setup than SQL Database but provides better performance for analytical queries and integration with data lake

resources.dependencyReasoning: Uses storage account as the default data lake for workspace and data storage

resources.name: sqlpool-sales-dwh-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Dedicated SQL Pool provides optimized data warehouse for analytical queries on processed sales data with columnstore indexing and MPP architecture.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Higher cost but provides significantly better performance for complex analytical queries and large datasets

resources.dependencyReasoning: Must be created within Synapse workspace as a child resource

references.title: Dedicated SQL pools in Azure Synapse Analytics

overallReasoning.summary: Modern data pipeline architecture implementing medallion pattern: Data Factory orchestrates daily data ingestion, Storage Account provides scalable data lake with hierarchical namespace, Databricks handles complex transformations with Spark processing, and Synapse Analytics provides enterprise data warehouse capabilities for analytical workloads.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Higher operational cost but provides enterprise-grade scalability, monitoring, security, and analytics capabilities. All services support managed identity for secure authentication and are optimized for big data workloads.

plan.validation: All resources are deployed in the same region (eastus2) for optimal performance and reduced data transfer costs. Dependencies are correctly ordered: Storage Account → Data Factory/Databricks → Synapse Workspace → SQL Pool. All resources support the daily ETL workflow with proper integration points.
